editor_settings.title: RTX 4090 24GB: Is It Still the Best GPU for Gaming and AI Work?
editor_settings.content:
RTX 4090 24GB: Is It Still the Best GPU for Gaming and AI Work?

The RTX 4090 24GB is still a monster.

But it is no longer the automatic king of everything.

In 2026, the honest answer is this: the RTX 4090 is still one of the best GPUs you can own, but the RTX 5090 has taken the top seat in some workloads.

Why The RTX 4090 Still Matters

NVIDIA built the RTX 4090 around Ada Lovelace, DLSS 3, ray tracing, creator features, and 24GB of GDDR6X memory.

That 24GB is the reason the card keeps aging better than normal gaming GPUs.

It helps with 4K gaming, large creative projects, Blender scenes, video workflows, and local AI experiments that simply need more VRAM.

The 4090 Still Feels Like A Beast

This AORUS unboxing captures the way people still talk about the card: 4K gaming, content creation, DLSS 3, ray tracing, cooling, and future-proofing.

@grigobyte 📦 Unboxing the beastly and stunning @NVIDIA GeForce GeForce RTX 4090 @AORUS @GIGABYTE AORUS MASTER. This GPU is made for those who demand nothing but the best - whether you’re gaming at 4K, creating content, or pushing your system to its limits. With features like 24 GB of GDDR6X memory, real-time ray tracing, and AI-driven DLSS 3.0, this card offers next-level performance and visual fidelity. Its powerful cooling system ensures it stays cool even during the most intense gaming sessions. If you’re serious about performance and future-proofing your build, this is the upgrade you need! What do you think about the RTX 4090 graphics card? Let us know below. #pc #pcbuild #pcsetup #gamingpc #tech #computer #gpu #graphicscard #aorus #gigabyte #nvidia #geforce ♬ BABYDOLL (Speed) - Ari Abdul

I would be careful with the word future-proof, but the feeling is real. The RTX 4090 still gives people more headroom than almost any GPU from its generation.

Where RTX 5090 Has Passed It

The RTX 5090 is faster in top-end compute and AI-style workloads.

A 2026 tweet comparing RTX 5090 and RTX 4090 in MD5 hash benchmarking shows exactly why the conversation changed.

NVIDIA’s RTX 5090 cracks MD5 password hashes at 220 GH/s and was tested on 231 million unique leaked passwords collected by Kaspersky from the dark web.

>One card cracked 48% in under one minute
>60% in less than one hour
>77% in one year

The speed gain over the RTX 4090 is… pic.twitter.com/exTm4pQvnF

— Raunak Yadush (@raunak_yadush) May 11, 2026

Use that safely as a benchmark signal, not a how-to. The point is that RTX 5090 has a clear compute lead in some tasks. The RTX 4090 is still strong, but it is not the ceiling anymore.

Gaming Verdict

For gaming, the RTX 4090 is still excellent.

At 4K, with ray tracing and DLSS 3, it remains far beyond what most people need. The problem is not performance. The problem is price.

If you already own one, there is no need to panic-upgrade for normal gaming.

AI And Creator Verdict

For local AI, 3D work, rendering, and creator workflows, 24GB still matters.

But if your workload scales hard with the newest GPU architecture, the 5090 can be the better tool. That is especially true if your work time has real money attached to it.

If you are shopping new in 2026, compare the real 4090 price against live 5090 options:

[product-card:asus-tuf-gaming-oc-geforce-rtx-5090-32gb-graphics-card-32gb-gddr7-pcie-50-21760-cuda-cores-8k-support-triple-fan-cooling]


Final Verdict

The RTX 4090 24GB is still one of the best GPUs for gaming and serious creator work.

It is not the absolute best anymore.

Buy it if the price is meaningfully below the RTX 5090 and you need 24GB. Skip it if the seller wants near-5090 money for a used 4090.
